commit_scope: Add a Form-native ELF64/aarch64 object emitter and wire macOS/Android Form-native object floors into target proof.

commands: git fetch origin main && git worktree add ~/.claude-worktrees/Coherence-Network/form-native-arm64-object-floor-20260614 -b codex/form-native-arm64-object-floor-20260614 origin/main

bash scripts/agent-coord.sh claim form-native-arm64-object-floor "walk next Form-native JIT/object floor: emit and inspect macOS Mach-O plus Android ELF arm64 objects without clang as compiler or assembler"
cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/form-asm.fk form-stdlib/form-lower.fk form-stdlib/form-elf.fk form-stdlib/tests/form-elf-band.fk
local_validation.notes: Focused ELF proof returns 31 with fourth arm: 1 band(s) four-way and 1 ok, 0 divergent. Hati target catalog proof returns 8191 with fourth arm included. The metal-plane audit writes Form-emitted object bytes to disk, inspects a Mach-O 64-bit object arm64 and an ELF 64-bit LSB relocatable ARM aarch64 object with file(1), and still runs clang only as oracle for broader BML/Hati and Form JIT C surfaces. A first audit attempt exposed a source-dialect driver issue from loading raw core.fk; the script was repaired to use the compiled core artifact and the final audit passed. Rebasing over the literal bp and token recipe floors made the combined full Form validation floor fourth arm: 148 band(s) four-way and 566 ok, 0 divergent.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push, PR, and GitHub checks.

deploy_validation.notes: No public deploy performed for this Form-native object-floor slice.

phase_gate.reason: Local Form proof, object inspection, and full validation passed; CI, review, merge, and deploy remain pending.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: Form now emits target object-format bytes for both supported arm64 metal planes: form-macho writes a macOS Mach-O relocatable object and form-elf writes an Android ELF64/aarch64 relocatable object from Form-lowered arm64 machine code without clang as compiler or assembler. The Hati target catalog exposes the per-target Form-native object emitter while keeping clang as an oracle/sample source for broader C surfaces.
test_flows: Lower the same arm64 Form program into machine-code bytes, wrap it with form-macho, write the hex as an object, and verify file(1) reports Mach-O 64-bit object arm64.
Lower the same arm64 Form program into machine-code bytes, wrap it with form-elf, write the hex as an object, and verify file(1) reports ELF 64-bit LSB relocatable ARM aarch64.
Validate form-elf-band and hati-os-targets-band through fkwu as the fourth sibling, then run the full Form suite with zero divergent bands.
